{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: notes
Each entry gives the derivative of the **column quantity**
with respect to the **row quantity**;
that is, the entry in row $i$ and column $j$ is
$\derivf{(\text{column } j \text{ quantity})}{(\text{row } i \text{ quantity})}$.

When differentiating a scalar with respect to a vector ($\vx$ or $\vb$),
the result is a vector of partial derivatives.
When differentiating a vector with respect to itself,
the result is the identity matrix $\idmat$.
:::

:::{#tbl-logistic-deriv-matrix}

$$
\begin{array}{r|ccccc}
 & \prob & \odds & \logodds & \vx & \vb \\
\hline
\prob
  & 1
  & \dfrac{1}{\sqf{1-\prob}}
  & \dfrac{1}{\prob(1-\prob)}
  & \mathbf{0}
  & \mathbf{0} \\[10pt]
\odds
  & \sqf{1-\prob}
  & 1
  & \dfrac{1}{\odds}
  & \mathbf{0}
  & \mathbf{0} \\[10pt]
\logodds
  & \prob(1-\prob)
  & \odds
  & 1
  & \mathbf{0}
  & \mathbf{0} \\[10pt]
\vx
  & \prob(1-\prob)\,\vb
  & \odds\,\vb
  & \vb
  & \idmat
  & \mathbf{0} \\[10pt]
\vb
  & \prob(1-\prob)\,\vx
  & \odds\,\vx
  & \vx
  & \mathbf{0}
  & \idmat \\
\end{array}
$$

Matrix of logistic regression derivatives.
Each entry is the derivative of the **column quantity**
with respect to the **row quantity**.
:::
